mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above the neutral zone and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, markets price fewer near-term cuts and more persistent inflation risk, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL is flat-to-rising versus 3 months ago
failure_condition: If triggered instances do not show a positive 20-day Treasury yield change more often than not, or if yields fall despite FEDFUNDS staying above 4.0% and CPIAUCSL not decelerating, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS signals stronger nominal demand and better growth momentum, which lifts real-rate expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury yield changes are not positive on average when RSAFS is trending up over the prior 3 months, or if the sign flips repeatedly across expansionary samples.

mechanism_narrative: If UNRATE jumps while PAYEMS stalls or declines, the labor market is weakening fast enough to raise recession odds, which lowers Treasury term yields over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over 2 months and PAYEMS is below its level 2 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-weakening episodes fail to produce negative 20-day Treasury yield moves, or if yields rise in a majority of cases despite rising unemployment and falling payrolls.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial activity and capacity utilization, which raises growth expectations and nudges the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds its prior 3-month average by at least 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon yield changes are not positive after INDPRO breakouts, or if the effect disappears after controlling for concurrent CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS stability.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts contraction signals weaker rate-sensitive demand and softer forward growth, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% versus its level 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by negative 20-day Treasury yield changes, or if the sign is persistently positive during housing contractions.

mechanism_narrative: During an active tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with firm CPIAUCSL forces the market to price a higher average policy path, lifting the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 60-day yield response is not positive across tightening episodes, or if yields fall when FEDFUNDS is rising and CPIAUCSL remains above its recent trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS marks demand deceleration, so investors seek duration and the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below its prior-quarter pace and RSAFS is declining year over year
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-and-sales slowdowns do not predict negative 20-day yield moves, or if the signal is neutral once both series weaken together.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, unchanged UNRATE with continued PAYEMS growth signals durable employment without recession stress, which supports a modest rise in the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is unchanged or lower over 2 months and PAYEMS is still rising
failure_condition: Reject if soft-landing labor episodes do not produce positive 5-day yield moves, or if yields decline even while unemployment stays stable and payrolls continue to expand.
